name: Venice
localTips: Buy a multi-day vaporetto pass if you're staying more than two days — single tickets run about €9.50 each and the water bus is how everyone, including locals, gets around.
'Acqua alta' (high tide flooding) happens mostly November–January — free raised walkways go up in Piazza San Marco when it hits, and rubber boots are sold everywhere for a few euros.
Sitting down at a café table on Piazza San Marco can cost 4–5x the counter price once the 'servizio' and orchestra charge hit the bill — check the menu's table price before you sit.
Venice charges a day-tripper access fee (a few euros, booked online) on certain peak dates if you're not staying overnight — check the current calendar before you visit if it's a day trip.
Skip the gondola touts near San Marco and walk a few bridges into a quieter sestiere — the price is fixed citywide, but the ride is calmer and cheaper-feeling away from the crowds.

activities.title: Piazza San Marco
activities.description: Napoleon's 'drawing room of Europe' — the basilica's gilded domes, the Campanile and the Doge's Palace ringed by arcaded cafés, free to stand in even if every building charges to enter.

activities.title: Rialto Bridge
activities.description: The oldest bridge across the Grand Canal, its stone arch lined with jewelry shops since 1591 — free to cross, and the classic photo spot for the Grand Canal's curve in both directions.

activities.title: Mercato di Rialto
activities.description: Fishmongers shouting over crushed ice and produce stalls piled with radicchio and artichokes — Venice's working market has traded on this spot for over 1,000 years, free to wander before noon.

activities.title: Cannaregio & the Jewish Ghetto
activities.description: The world's original 'ghetto' (from the Venetian word for foundry) — tall, tightly packed buildings, five historic synagogues and a quiet square that gave the term its meaning in 1516.

activities.title: Fondamenta della Misericordia
activities.description: A canal-side strip in Cannaregio where Venetians actually go out — bacari and bars spill onto the fondamenta with spritz in hand, none of the San Marco markup.

activities.title: Campo Santa Margherita
activities.description: A broad, market-stalled square ringed by university bars — Venice's most lived-in piazza, full of students and locals rather than day-trippers, day and night.

activities.title: Bridge of Sighs viewpoint
activities.description: Byron named it for the last sighs of prisoners crossing from the Doge's Palace interrogation rooms to jail — the enclosed limestone bridge is best seen (free) from Ponte della Paglia.

activities.title: Squero di San Trovaso
activities.description: One of the last working gondola boatyards in Venice — a wooden Alpine-style shed where craftsmen still build and repair gondolas by hand, viewable free from the opposite canal bank.

activities.title: San Giorgio Maggiore church
activities.description: Palladio's white marble church on its own island across the lagoon from San Marco — free to enter, with Tintorettos inside and the postcard skyline view of Venice right from its steps.

activities.title: Basilica di Santa Maria della Salute
activities.description: A vast white-domed church built as a votive offering after the 1630 plague, guarding the mouth of the Grand Canal — free to enter, with a small sacristy fee for the Titians inside.

activities.title: Arsenale gates & walls
activities.description: The medieval super-shipyard that built a warship a day at its peak, guarded by stone lions looted from Piraeus — free to walk the perimeter and Renaissance land gate.

activities.title: Dorsoduro backstreets & canals
activities.description: Quiet, sun-washed calli away from the San Marco crush — laundry strung over narrow canals, art students sketching, and far fewer selfie sticks than anywhere else in the city center.

activities.title: Ponte dei Pugni
activities.description: The 'Bridge of Fists' where rival Venetian factions once brawled with sticks until someone fell in the canal — four marble footprints on the parapet still mark the old fighting stances.

activities.title: Libreria Acqua Alta
activities.description: Books piled inside bathtubs and a full-size gondola to survive the next flood — a chaotic, cat-patrolled bookshop that's become one of Venice's most photographed hidden spots.
activities.insiderNote: Climb the staircase made of waterlogged encyclopedias out back to a canal-view terrace most visitors never find — it's free, just past the main room.

activities.title: Bacari crawl: cicchetti & ombra
activities.description: Hop between tiny standing-room wine bars in San Polo and Cannaregio for cicchetti (bar snacks) and an 'ombra' — a small glass of wine, named for the shade the bell tower once cast over the original stall.
activities.insiderNote: Order like a local: point at 2–3 cicchetti and an ombra, eat standing at the counter, then move to the next bacaro rather than sitting down at one — that's the whole ritual.

activities.title: Original Bellini at Harry's Bar
activities.description: Giuseppe Cipriani invented the peach-and-prosecco Bellini here in 1948, and Hemingway drank at the same small bar counter — a slice of old Venice glamour a few steps off San Marco.
activities.insiderNote: Stand at the bar rather than sit at a table — same drink, a fraction of the seated price, and it's genuinely how the regulars order it.

activities.title: Coffee at Caffè Florian
activities.description: Open since 1720, it's the oldest continuously operating café in the world — Casanova, Byron and Dickens all drank here under frescoed, gilt-mirrored rooms on Piazza San Marco.
activities.insiderNote: Order at the counter instead of a piazza table to skip the orchestra surcharge — the mirrored back rooms are free to peek into even without ordering.

activities.title: Mask-painting workshop at Ca' Macana
activities.description: The mask atelier that made the leather masks for Kubrick's Eyes Wide Shut — a hands-on papier-mâché painting class in a working Dorsoduro studio, not a souvenir shop.
activities.insiderNote: Book the smaller morning workshop slot — afternoons get filled with cruise-ship groups, and the artisans have more time to talk technique when it's quieter.

activities.title: Standing-room wine at Osteria al Squero
activities.description: A closet-sized bacaro directly across the canal from the San Trovaso gondola boatyard — no seats, just a crowd spilling onto the fondamenta with cicchetti and a glass of wine at sunset.
activities.insiderNote: There's no indoor seating at all — buy your ombra and stand on the fondamenta facing the squero; go right at golden hour for boat-builders still working across the water.

activities.title: Campo San Giacomo dell'Orio
activities.description: A leafy, laid-back square in Santa Croce where local families actually eat dinner outside — one of the few campi in central Venice with more residents than tourists at any hour.
activities.insiderNote: The trattorias facing the square are priced for locals, not day-trippers — a genuinely rare thing within ten minutes of the Rialto.

activities.title: Torcello, the original Venice
activities.description: Once home to more people than Venice itself, now down to a handful of residents — a Byzantine cathedral with a golden mosaic and 'Attila's Throne' sit almost alone in tall grass.
activities.insiderNote: Take vaporetto line 9 from Burano rather than direct — it's cheaper and quieter, and the island empties out completely an hour before the last boat back.

activities.title: Giudecca Island waterfront
activities.description: A five-minute vaporetto hop from San Marco but a world apart — a residential island of gardens and quiet fondamente with the single best skyline view looking back at Venice itself.
activities.insiderNote: Walk the southern fondamenta at golden hour for the classic postcard view of San Marco's domes across the water, with almost nobody else around.

activities.title: Night vaporetto down the Grand Canal
activities.description: Line 1 at night is the budget gondola: floodlit palazzi sliding past from a covered outdoor deck for the price of a regular transit ticket, no crowds and no upsell.
activities.insiderNote: Ride the full length from Piazzale Roma to San Marco after dark and stand at the open back deck — it's the same route as a €90 gondola tour for a €9.50 ticket.

activities.title: St Mark's Basilica
activities.description: Byzantine domes covered in 8,000 square meters of gold mosaic, looted Constantinople treasures and the bronze horses above the entrance — book a skip-the-line slot, the queue is brutal.
activities.bookingQuery: St Marks Basilica skip the line ticket Venice

activities.title: Doge's Palace
activities.description: The seat of Venetian power for 700 years — the Great Council Hall's giant Tintoretto Paradiso, gilded ceilings, and the prison cells across the Bridge of Sighs.

activities.title: Classic gondola ride
activities.description: Thirty minutes gliding through narrow back canals under low bridges, steered by a single oar — the quintessential Venice experience, fixed-price and worth doing once.

activities.title: Peggy Guggenheim Collection
activities.description: The American heiress's unfinished palazzo on the Grand Canal, packed with Pollock, Picasso and Dalí she personally collected and once lived among — a garden café and sculpture terrace too.

activities.title: Gallerie dell'Accademia
activities.description: The finest collection of Venetian painting anywhere — Titian, Bellini, Veronese and Tintoretto in the former convent that trained them, plus Leonardo's Vitruvian Man on rotating display.

activities.title: Scuola Grande di San Rocco
activities.description: Sixty Tintorettos cover every wall and ceiling of this confraternity hall — the artist worked here for 23 years; hand mirrors are provided so you can study the ceiling without a stiff neck.
activities.bookingQuery: Scuola Grande di San Rocco ticket Venice

activities.title: Murano glass-blowing demonstration
activities.description: A short vaporetto ride to the island where Venetian glassmakers were exiled in 1291 (fire risk) — watch a furnace master shape molten glass, then browse showroom after showroom.
activities.bookingQuery: Murano glass blowing demonstration tour Venice

activities.title: Burano's rainbow houses
activities.description: Fishermen painted their houses in bold colors to spot home through fog — now a candy-colored island of lace-making workshops, 45 minutes from Venice by vaporetto.
activities.bookingQuery: Burano island day trip from Venice

activities.title: Teatro La Fenice opera or tour
activities.description: 'The Phoenix' has burned down and been rebuilt twice — hear Verdi-era acoustics at an evening opera, or tour the gilded, cherub-covered auditorium by day.
activities.bookingQuery: Teatro La Fenice opera tickets or tour Venice

activities.title: Campanile di San Marco bell tower
activities.description: Venice's tallest structure, rebuilt in 1912 after collapsing — an elevator (no stairs) whisks you 98.6 meters up for the definitive view of the lagoon, rooftops and mainland Alps on a clear day.
activities.bookingQuery: Campanile di San Marco bell tower ticket Venice

activities.title: Santa Maria Gloriosa dei Frari
activities.description: A vast Gothic brick church holding Titian's towering Assumption altarpiece and his own tomb — one of Venice's greatest art collections, hidden inside a working parish church.
activities.bookingQuery: Basilica dei Frari entry ticket Venice

activities.title: Grand Canal vaporetto day pass
activities.description: A full day of unlimited water-bus travel — ride the length of the Grand Canal past 50 palazzi, hop to the islands, and use it as your entire transit system at once.
